title: Music from Around the World is Getting Popular on Spotify
summary: Spotify, a music streaming app, says that songs in many languages are becoming more popular. English used to be the most common language in music, but now songs in Spanish, Korean, Portuguese, and other languages are in the top charts. Bad Bunny, who sings in Spanish, was the most-streamed artist in the world. Brazilian Funk, K-Pop, and Trap Latino are growing fast and earned a lot of money. English still has many popular albums, but fans are listening to more music from different countries. Spotify paid artists billions of dollars last year, helping musicians earn money from their songs.

keywords.explanation: a music streaming app where people listen to songs
listening to music online without downloading it
lists that show the most popular songs
different ways people speak, like English or Spanish
money paid to artists when their songs are played
types of music, like pop or funk

questions.question: Which artist was the most-streamed in the world?
What language does Bad Bunny sing in?
Which genre grew the fastest on Spotify?
How many languages were in Spotify's Global Top 50 last year?

background_read: Music streaming lets people listen to songs online. Spotify is a popular app for this. Artists from different countries make music in their own languages. When songs are played, artists earn money called royalties. Charts show which songs are most popular. Recently, music from places like South Korea and Brazil is becoming more popular worldwide, showing that people enjoy diverse sounds.
Article_Structure: WHO: Spotify, artists like Bad Bunny, and music fans
WHAT: More songs in different languages are popular on Spotify, and English music is less dominant
WHERE: Global, with examples from countries like Puerto Rico, South Korea, and Brazil
WHY: Because fans are exploring music from around the world, and streaming helps artists earn money

perspectives.description: The article shows that music from many languages and countries is becoming more popular, making the world's music scene richer.
